summaries.brief_summary: Release v2.15.0 shipped with per-operation RWF flags and a liburing 2.15 update, plus fixes for opam sandbox builds and Eio compatibility.
summaries.new_features_summary: Per-operation RWF flags for read/write ops and vendored liburing bumped to 2.15, released as v2.15.0.
summaries.new_features:
- New `Rw_flags` module exposes per-operation `preadv2`/`pwritev2` flags (`hipri`, `dsync`, `sync`, `nowait`, `append`, `noappend`, `atomic`, `dontcache`) and threads an optional `?flags` argument through `read`, `write`, `readv`, `writev`, `read_fixed`, `read_chunk`, `write_fixed` and `write_chunk`, letting callers request per-write durability (`dsync`/`sync`) or drop pages from the cache after streaming I/O (`dontcache`) without a separate `fsync` call, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154))
- Vendored `liburing` updated to 2.15, pulling in a new `bpf_filter.h` header and the corresponding `liburing.so.2.15` shared library into the `dune` build rules, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157))
- `FLAGS.t` restored to `private int` (rather than made fully abstract) so that existing code depending on the underlying integer representation, such as Eio, keeps working ([ocaml-multicore/eio#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)), by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159))
- Fixed the vendored `configure` test to avoid writing to `/tmp`, which opam's build sandbox marks read-only and had broken installation of `uring.dev`, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158))
- Release [v2.15.0](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/releases/tag/v2.15.0) published, bundling this week's `Rw_flags` and liburing 2.15 work together with earlier accumulated new ops and bug fixes, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)
- `README.md` subtree-pull instructions updated to reference the `liburing-2.15` tag, and `tests/main.md` gained a worked example showing `dsync` writes, flag-free `readv`, and the `EOPNOTSUPP` fallback path when an unsupported flag bit is set, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154), [#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157))
summaries.activity_summary: Three PRs opened and merged within the week for build sandboxing and FLAGS.t compatibility, alongside the RWF flags work and the v2.15.0 release.
summaries.activity:
- All three PRs opened this week were merged the same week: the liburing 2.15 update by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157)), followed in quick succession by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)'s opam-sandbox build fix ([#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)) and `FLAGS.t` compatibility revert ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159)), both merged within a day of opening.
- The per-operation `RWF` flags PR opened in the prior week by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) was merged this week ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154)), rounding out the API work that fed into the [v2.15.0](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/releases/tag/v2.15.0) release published by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm).
- Discussion continued on the open issue about building without vendored `liburing`, with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) explaining the opam-distribution rationale and exploring an optional environment-variable toggle for a future version ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
summaries.discussions_summary: Ongoing debate on issue #156 over vendoring liburing versus depending on a system-provided version.
summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) argued against vendoring `liburing` and asked for a way to build against the system library, citing a Debian 13 packaging mismatch on the minimum required version; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) responded that vendoring is important for opam distribution while `liburing` remains unavailable on several distros, noted he explored an optional environment-variable toggle to decouple it but found it messy, and agreed with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)'s suggestion that a packaging-layer patch is the best near-term fix ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
- Review on the liburing 2.15 update surfaced an opam sandbox build failure (`mktemp` failing against a read-only `/tmp`) caught by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) shortly after merge, resolved via a follow-up fix that was folded into the same buildable history ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157), [#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)).
summaries.trends_summary: Continued emphasis on Eio compatibility and opam sandbox build robustness while adopting newer liburing/io_uring capabilities.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- Care to preserve downstream Eio compatibility when evolving the API surface, as seen in the `FLAGS.t` revert to keep the type's integer representation available ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159), [ocaml-multicore/eio#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)).
- Attention to opam's sandboxed build environment, with a second `/tmp`-related build fix landing this week after the liburing 2.15 vendor update exposed the issue ([#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)).
- Incremental exposure of newer Linux `io_uring`/`liburing` capabilities at the OCaml layer, from per-operation `RWF` flags to the BPF filter header pulled in with liburing 2.15 ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154), [#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157)).
summaries.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy shipped RWF flags, liburing 2.15, and the release; Thomas Leonard fixed a build bug; Stéphane Glondu raised vendoring concerns.
summaries.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) implemented the `Rw_flags` API for per-operation read/write flags ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154)), updated the vendored `liburing` to 2.15 including the new BPF filter header ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157)), published the [v2.15.0](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/releases/tag/v2.15.0) release, and responded to [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu)'s vendoring concerns on issue [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156).
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reviewed the liburing 2.15 update and caught an opam sandbox build failure, fixing the vendored `configure` test's use of `/tmp` ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157), [#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)), restored `FLAGS.t`'s private-int representation to avoid breaking Eio ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159)), and commented on the vendoring discussion in [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156).
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened the discussion on issue [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156) requesting a way to build without vendored `liburing`, explaining the Debian packaging constraint that motivated the request.

summaries.brief_summary: Vectored fixed-buffer read/write bindings merged; RWF flags PR and vendored-liburing discussion remain open.
summaries.new_features_summary: Added vectored `readv_fixed`/`writev_fixed` bindings for fixed buffers and fixed misnamed C stubs ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155)).
summaries.new_features:
- Added `readv_fixed` and `writev_fixed` to the `Uring` API, letting callers submit multi-buffer reads and writes against a ring's registered **fixed buffer** in a single operation, analogous to the existing `read_fixed`/`write_fixed` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- Added validation that raises `Invalid_argument` if any buffer passed to `readv_fixed`/`writev_fixed` does not belong to the ring's fixed buffer, guarding against misuse before submission ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- Fixed a naming bug where the existing `read_fixed`/`write_fixed` functions called into C stubs named `submit_readv_fixed`/`submit_writev_fixed` without ever performing vectored I/O; renamed to `submit_read_fixed`/`submit_write_fixed` so the vectored names became available for the genuinely vectored operations ([56699618](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/commit/56699618217cb588a1045ac43d1727e312544998))
- Extended `tests/main.md` with examples exercising the new vectored fixed-buffer operations ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
summaries.activity_summary: PR #155 merged for vectored fixed-buffer bindings; RWF flags PR #154 and vendored-liburing issue #156 remain active.
summaries.activity:
- The **vectored fixed-buffer bindings** work was completed and merged by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), correcting the misnamed stubs along the way, with review approval from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- The **per-operation RWF flags** PR by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), which exposes `preadv2`/`pwritev2`-style flags such as `dsync` and `nocache` at the uring layer for later use in Eio_linux, remains open; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) asked for a rebase now that the fixed-buffer bindings landed, and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) reviewed the changes favorably ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154))
- Discussion continues on whether the package should support building against **system `liburing`** instead of only the vendored copy, following a build failure on Debian caused by a missing `io_uring_prep_readv_fixed` declaration ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
summaries.discussions_summary: Debate over supporting system liburing instead of the vendored copy, prompted by a Debian build failure ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
summaries.notable_discussions: - [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) discussed whether to support building against system `liburing` rather than the vendored copy; Debian's patched build fails with an implicit-declaration error for `io_uring_prep_readv_fixed`, while opam-installed `uring` often needs a newer `liburing` than stable distributions ship, illustrating the tension between packaging stability and access to newer kernel features ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
summaries.trends_summary: Fixed-buffer/vectored I/O API expansion continues alongside packaging concerns about liburing versioning ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154), [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155), [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
summaries.emerging_trends:
- Continued work around **fixed/registered buffer operations** — first correcting stub naming and then adding vectored variants — points to an ongoing effort to broaden zero-copy I/O primitive coverage ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154), [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- Packaging and distribution concerns around **vendored vs system `liburing`** are surfacing as the library adopts kernel-version-dependent features, since newer operations require `liburing` versions ahead of what stable distributions package ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
summaries.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy merged the vectored fixed-buffer bindings and continued the RWF flags PR, with review input from Thomas Leonard and Patrick Ferris, and packaging discussion from Stéphane Glondu.
summaries.contributors:

summaries.brief_summary: Discussion continued on a draft PR converting buffers to bytes, and a new issue requested system liburing support.
summaries.activity_summary: PR #153's buffer redesign drew review feedback while issue #156 raised building without vendored liburing.
summaries.activity:
- Draft PR converting the **buffer representation from bigarray/cstruct to bytes** by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) continued to receive review feedback this week, with discussion of buffer-size validation and safety trade-offs for reused buffers ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/153))
- New issue opened by [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) asking for a way to build against a **system liburing** instead of the vendored copy, prompting a response from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) on why the vendored version is pinned to a specific kernel ABI ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
summaries.discussions_summary: Reviewers debated buffer-safety trade-offs in the bytes-based buffer PR and the rationale for vendoring liburing.
summaries.notable_discussions:
- On the **bytes-based buffer** draft PR, [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) raised concerns that reusing a buffer without zeroing it could leak private data unless exposed data is limited to what was actually filled, suggesting a `create_unsafe` variant and an `Iovec.to_cstruct` helper that shares the underlying memory without copying ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/153))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) responded that requiring caller-provided buffers of 2KB+ avoids the leak concern, since `uring` only needs to verify incoming buffer size rather than manage the memory itself ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/153))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) explained the rationale for **vendoring liburing** — testing against a specific version tied to the Linux kernel ABI — in response to a request for system-library support, noting that Debian's unstable liburing currently matches the vendored version as a short-term workaround ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
summaries.contributors_summary: avsm advanced the buffer-type PR and answered a vendoring question; talex5 reviewed the PR; glondu opened a new issue.
summaries.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened and maintained the draft PR switching buffer handling from bigarray/cstruct to bytes, responding to review feedback on buffer safety ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/153)), and answered a question about the rationale for vendoring liburing on the issue tracker ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reviewed the buffer-type PR, flagging a potential data-leak concern with buffer reuse and proposing API alternatives ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/153))
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened an issue requesting the ability to build against system liburing instead of the vendored copy ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))

summaries.brief_summary: Socket creation now defaults to CLOEXEC; new PRs add RWF flags and vectored I/O bindings to ocaml-uring.
summaries.new_features_summary: Socket CLOEXEC default added; RWF per-operation flags and vectored fixed-buffer bindings proposed.
summaries.new_features:
- **Socket creation now defaults to `CLOEXEC`** in [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151): the `socket` function's `?flags` optional argument was changed from `Sock_flags.empty` to `Sock_flags.cloexec`, preventing accidental fd leaks into child processes without requiring a separate `set_close_on_exec` call. The API change is backwards-compatible — callers who want no flags can pass `~flags:Sock_flags.empty` explicitly.
- **Per-operation RWF flags for read/write submissions** proposed in [#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154): exposes the Linux `preadv2`/`pwritev2` flags (e.g. `RWF_DSYNC` to avoid a separate `fsync` after writes, and `RWF_NOCACHE` to skip polluting the buffer cache during large copies). These flags are available only on recent 6.x kernels and are intended for use in `eio_linux`.
- **Vectored read/write bindings for fixed buffers** proposed in [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155): corrects previously misnamed C stubs that were not actually performing vectored I/O, and adds proper `readv`/`writev` bindings for fixed-buffer operations.
summaries.activity_summary: Three new PRs opened for low-level I/O improvements; two older experimental PRs closed; bytes buffer migration ongoing.
summaries.activity:
- **New PR [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155)** opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): adds correct vectored read/writev bindings for fixed buffers, fixing previously misnamed C stubs that were not performing vectored calls (172 additions, 20 deletions across 5 files).
- **New PR [#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154)** opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): exposes `preadv2`/`pwritev2` RWF flags at the uring layer, enabling callers like `eio_linux` to use `RWF_DSYNC` and `RWF_NOCACHE` to reduce unnecessary kernel operations.
- **Draft PR [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)** switching the buffer type from `bigarray`/`cstruct` to `bytes` is active with ongoing benchmarking: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) shared microbenchmark results showing no regression in filesystem I/O throughput, improved large-buffer allocation (avoids bigarray finalizer overhead), but worse small-buffer performance due to a 2KB minimum allocation floor needed to keep bytes immovable.
- **PR [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151)** merged by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): socket flags support landed with CLOEXEC as the new default, enabling [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843) to use uring socket creation directly.
- **PR [#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152)** (Linux errno module) closed without merging: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noted the module has limited utility in Eio since `Unix_error` is always required, and suggested adding useful errnos upstream to OCaml instead. Discussion referenced [ocaml-multicore/eio#788](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/788) and [ocaml-multicore/eio#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851).
- **PR [#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101)** (Cstruct-to-Bstruct experiment) closed by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) in favour of the more comprehensive bytes migration in [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153).
summaries.discussions_summary: Benchmarking tradeoffs of bytes vs bigarray and errno module utility debated across PRs #152 and #153.
summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Bytes vs bigarray benchmarking** in [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) asked whether the switch to `bytes` showed measurable improvement; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) shared results showing improved large-buffer allocation and I/O throughput when the caller already uses bytes (avoiding `Cstruct.to_bytes` conversion), but noted small buffers are worse due to the 2KB minimum allocation needed to prevent GC movement. The tradeoff suggests callers should coalesce small writes before submitting.
- **Errno module utility** in [#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152): extensive discussion between [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) on whether a Linux-specific errno module adds value given that `Unix_error` is always required for Eio's `Low_level` fallback paths. The conclusion was to close the PR and revisit only if specific errnos prove necessary, with a preference for adding useful ones upstream to OCaml rather than in ocaml-uring.
summaries.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to modernize ocaml-uring's API: safer defaults, bytes-backed buffers, and finer-grained Linux I/O control.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **API modernization toward safer defaults**: the CLOEXEC change in [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151) reflects a pattern of making secure behavior opt-out rather than opt-in, aligning with modern Linux socket best practices.
- **Migration away from Cstruct/bigarray toward OCaml `bytes`**: [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) and the closure of [#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101) show a deliberate effort to reduce dependencies on C-backed memory types and improve interoperability with standard OCaml libraries that work with `bytes`.
- **Exposing more fine-grained Linux kernel features**: [#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154) (RWF flags) and [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155) (vectored fixed-buffer I/O) continue the pattern of surfacing advanced `io_uring` and Linux kernel capabilities at the OCaml level for use by higher-level libraries like `eio_linux`.
summaries.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy drove all code changes this week; Thomas Leonard and Patrick Ferris contributed review and triage.
summaries.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) merged [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151) (socket CLOEXEC default), opened new PRs [#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154) (RWF flags) and [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155) (vectored fixed-buffer bindings), continued work on draft [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) (bytes buffer migration) including sharing benchmark results, and participated in closing discussions for [#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152).
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) provided review feedback on [#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152) (questioning the utility of the errno module and referencing [ocaml-multicore/eio#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851) as an alternative), asked clarifying questions on [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) about benchmarking results, and discussed socket CLOEXEC default behavior in [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151).
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) closed the long-standing experimental PR [#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101) (Cstruct-to-Bstruct) in favour of the newer bytes migration approach in [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153).

summaries.brief_summary: Busy week adding fallocate, ftruncate, shutdown, socket, renameat, symlinkat bindings and fixing Statx API types.
summaries.new_features_summary: New uring bindings for fallocate, ftruncate, shutdown, socket, renameat, symlinkat; improved FLAGS and Statx APIs.
summaries.new_features:
- New **`fallocate` binding** with a `Fallocate_flags` module exposing `keep_size`, `punch_hole`, `collapse_range`, `zero_range`, `insert_range`, `unshare_range`, and `write_zeroes` flags, enabling async disk space manipulation ([#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149))
- New **`ftruncate` binding** for async file size adjustment via io_uring ([#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149))
- New **`shutdown` binding** providing an async version of `shutdown(2)` accepting `Unix.shutdown_command` ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- New **`socket` binding** for async socket creation, returning the new file descriptor through the completion result ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- New **`renameat` binding** with a `Rename_flags` module exposing `noreplace`, `exchange`, and `whiteout` flags for atomic renames and overlay-filesystem use ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- New **`symlinkat` binding** for async symlink creation with optional `dir_fd` for relative paths ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- **`io_uring_submit` errors** are now raised as exceptions instead of returning a raw negative integer, improving error propagation ([#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144))
- **`Statx.Attr` API** overhauled: the inverted `check` function is replaced by a correctly named `mem_checked ~mask` that takes typed `Attr.t` arguments; `attributes` and `attributes_mask` accessors now return `Attr.t` instead of `Int64.t` ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **`Statx.mask` accessor** now returns a typed `Mask.t` value instead of `Int64.t` ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **`FLAGS` module type** extended with `to_int` and a polymorphic `( = )` operator, and the `type t` is now abstract rather than `private int` ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **`Region.init`** simplified to calculate the number of slots automatically from the buffer size and slot size, removing the need to pass a count separately ([#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145))
- **`AT_FDCWD` handling** moved to C stubs, eliminating the use of `Obj.magic` in the OCaml layer ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128))
summaries.activity_summary: Three PRs remain open including an errno module, a bytes-based buffer redesign, and socket-flag support; eight PRs merged.
summaries.activity:
- **Newly opened** this week and still open: an **errno module** adding Linux-specific error codes by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152)); a **draft PR switching the buffer type from bigarray/cstruct to `bytes`** (surprisingly small diff) ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)); and a PR to **allow setting `CLOEXEC` on socket creation**, motivated by [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843), with discussion about whether this should be always-on ([#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151))
- **Filesystem operations** expanded: `fallocate` and `ftruncate` bindings added with kernel-version guards in tests for the very new `FALLOC_FL_WRITE_ZEROES` flag ([#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149)); `renameat` with rename flags hard-coded for older musl compatibility ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147)); `symlinkat` with corrected symlink-follow test ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- **Networking operations** expanded with `shutdown` and async `socket` creation bindings, reusing the OCaml runtime's socket table rather than redefining it ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- **Null CQE safety check** added to `caml_uring_wait_cqe` as a C `assert`, matching the existing check in the timeout variant ([#148](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/148))
- **Debug-mode build warnings** fixed: corrected `%p` format specifiers and errno sign in `ocaml_uring_unregister_buffers`, and additional `URING_DEBUG` format warnings across `uring_stubs.c` ([#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146))
- **Minimum OCaml version** raised to 5.2, removing a now-unnecessary compiler warning ([#150](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/150))
- **`lintcstubs-arity` depopt** added to the test dependency to ensure `primitives.h` regeneration is caught in CI ([#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143))
summaries.discussions_summary: Design debate on CLOEXEC socket flag defaulting and Statx mask semantics; Region.init API trade-off around underfilling buffers.
summaries.notable_discussions:
- Discussion on **whether `CLOEXEC` should be opt-in or always-on** for socket creation: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggested always setting it for safety, while [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) argued uring bindings should follow kernel defaults and that Eio can apply the flag at a higher level ([#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151))
- **`Statx.Attr` semantics** clarified during review of the inverted mask fix: discussion covered the distinction between `stx_mask` (fields filled) and `stx_attributes_mask` (supported attribute flags), and what it means for an attribute to be simultaneously unsupported and set ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **`Region.init` API design**: trade-off between allowing underfilled buffers vs. deriving slot count from buffer size, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggesting the simpler derived approach and noting that `Uring.Region` may eventually move into Eio entirely ([#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145))
- **`caml_uring_wait_cqe` null check**: debate on whether a C `assert` or a runtime OCaml check was more appropriate for a condition that should never occur in practice ([#148](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/148))
summaries.trends_summary: Systematic expansion of uring bindings to cover more Linux syscalls, with typed APIs replacing raw Int64 for Statx fields.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Typed API surfaces replacing raw integers**: the shift of `Statx.attributes`, `attributes_mask`, and `mask` from `Int64.t` to typed `Attr.t` / `Mask.t`, and making `FLAGS.t` fully abstract, reflects a push toward more compile-time–safe interfaces ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **Systematic syscall coverage**: adding `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, `symlinkat`, `fallocate`, and `ftruncate` in one week suggests a focused effort to bring uring bindings to feature parity with commonly needed Linux operations, likely driven by Eio requirements ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147), [#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149))
- **OxCaml compatibility** is an active concern: comments in [#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142) mention avoiding eta-reduction to satisfy OxCaml's stricter requirements and keeping types abstract for future `local` interactions
- **Buffer type migration**: the draft PR [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) to replace bigarray/cstruct with plain `bytes` signals a potential simplification of the buffer story
summaries.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy authored nearly all PRs; Thomas Leonard reviewed and co-authored fixes; David Allsopp contributed AT_FDCWD cleanup.

summaries.brief_summary: v2.14.0 released; AT_FDCWD handling moved to C eliminating Obj.magic; several bug fixes and new op PRs opened.
summaries.new_features_summary: AT_FDCWD now handled in C stubs; fd params changed to option type; taskrun_flag docs improved.
summaries.new_features:
- **`AT_FDCWD` handling moved to C stubs**, eliminating use of `Obj.magic` in the OCaml layer. Functions `openat2`, `linkat`, `unlink`, `mkdirat`, and `statx` now accept `Unix.file_descr option` where `None` maps to `AT_FDCWD` natively in C, via [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128). The `at_fdcwd` binding and discovery of `AT_FDCWD` via `discover.ml` are removed.
- Fixed **`dio_offset_align` statx getter** that was incorrectly returning `stx_dio_mem_align` instead of the correct `stx_dio_offset_align` field — a copy-paste error affecting direct I/O alignment queries ([#141](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/141)).
- Improved **`taskrun_flag` documentation** in `uring.mli`, now explaining its interaction with `coop_taskrun`, `defer_taskrun`, and `get_cqe_nonblocking`, including the `IORING_SQ_TASKRUN` kernel flag semantics ([4026e90](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/commit/4026e90aec66f2cba94f596813124dbb7853072f)).
- **v2.14.0 release** published by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), documenting breaking changes (`Uring.Res` module), new ops (`send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc`), and bug fixes from prior work ([#140](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/140)).
summaries.activity_summary: Several PRs opened for new io_uring ops and fixes; lintcstubs-arity CI dependency corrected; io_uring_submit error reporting improved.
summaries.activity:
- **New ops PR** opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) adding `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, and `symlinkat` operations with individual commits and tests, fixing the symlink-follow test for current liburing ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147)) — not yet merged.
- **Statx `Attr` API bug** found and PR opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): the supported check had an inverted condition, uncovered by new tests ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142)) — not yet merged.
- **`Region.init` bounds check** added to raise early on invalid parameters rather than failing on subsequent access, opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145)) — opened this week, merged the following week.
- **`io_uring_submit` error reporting** fixed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): previously a negative return value was silently passed through; now raises a Unix error ([#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144)) — opened this week, merged the following week.
- **Debug message fixes** in `ocaml_uring_unregister_buffers` correcting missing `%p` format args and wrong sign for errno, opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146)) — opened this week, merged the following week.
- **`lintcstubs-arity` dependency** changed from depopt to hard test dependency so CI reliably catches missing primitive regenerations; primitives header regenerated ([#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143)).
summaries.discussions_summary: PR #142 prompted a discussion about redesigning the Statx Attr API to use abstract types instead of int64.
summaries.notable_discussions:
- The **`Statx.Attr` API design** came under scrutiny in [#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggested the `check` function signature should use the abstract `t` type rather than raw `int64` arguments, and proposed aligning it with `Attr.mem`; he also asked [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) for input on the proposed change. [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) pushed a revised implementation making the type abstract and adding `Flags.(=)` and `of/to_int` conversions.
- In [#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143), [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) raised the question of whether `lintcstubs-arity` needs to remain optional at all, suggesting it could be optional in the dune file but mandatory in opam to simplify testing on unreleased compilers.
- In [#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) flagged that the `caml_unix_error` function is unavailable on OCaml 4.x and raised the question of dropping OCaml 4.x support for this library, given Eio only targets OCaml 5.x.
summaries.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to eliminate unsafe OCaml patterns (Obj.magic) and improve API type safety in the library.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- A sustained effort to **eliminate `Obj.magic`** from the codebase is visible across [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128) (AT_FDCWD), and is referenced as a motivating factor in [#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142) (Attr API abstraction). The library is progressively moving unsafe C interop into C stubs rather than OCaml-side casts.
- The **OCaml 5.x-only question** emerged in [#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144): since Eio (the primary consumer) requires OCaml 5.x, there is discussion about dropping OCaml 4.x compatibility, which would allow using `caml_unix_error` and other 5.x-only APIs directly.
- **New io_uring operation coverage** is expanding: `send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc` were part of the v2.14.0 release, and [#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147) adds `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, and `symlinkat`.
summaries.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy drove most new PRs and bug fixes; Thomas Leonard contributed the release, error reporting fix, and review feedback.
summaries.contributors:

summaries.brief_summary: liburing updated to 2.14, new zero-copy and io_uring ops exposed, GC registration bug fixed.
summaries.new_features_summary: New io_uring ops (send_zc, sendmsg_zc, recv_zc, epoll_wait, pipe, etc.) and Setup_flags (hybrid_iopoll, cqe_mixed, sqe_mixed) added.
summaries.new_features:
- Fixed **GC registration bug** in `ocaml_uring_setup` where the `polling_timeout` (`int option`) parameter was not registered with the GC via `CAMLparam`, which could cause memory corruption when the GC moves values; reported by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) ([#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135)) and fixed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136))
- Added **`send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` zero-copy send operations** (`IORING_OP_SEND_ZC`, `IORING_OP_SENDMSG_ZC`) to the capability discovery system in `discover.ml` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
- Added **seven further new io_uring operations** to `discover.ml` as part of the liburing 2.14 update: `IORING_OP_RECV_ZC`, `IORING_OP_EPOLL_WAIT`, `IORING_OP_READV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_WRITEV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_PIPE`, `IORING_OP_NOP128`, and `IORING_OP_URING_CMD128` by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Added **four new `Setup_flags`** to the OCaml API in `uring.mli`: `no_sqarray` (fail on older indirect submission systems), `hybrid_iopoll` (delayed I/O completion polling to reduce CPU usage), `cqe_mixed` (mixed 16/32-bit responses to save memory), and `sqe_mixed` (mixed 64/128-bit requests to save memory), with documentation pointing to `io_uring_setup_flags(7)` ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Updated **vendored liburing to version 2.14** (from the previous version), incorporating 305 changed files with new operations, examples, tests, and man pages for features like `io_uring_resize_rings`, `io_uring_register_region`, zero-copy receive (`zcrx`), and `io_uring_submit_and_wait_min_timeout` by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Updated **README.md documentation** with instructions for updating liburing to new versions, covering the `git subtree pull` workflow and required edits to `dune` and `discover.ml` ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137))
summaries.activity_summary: All four PRs opened this week were merged: GC bug fix, build docs, liburing 2.14 update, and new zero-copy ops.
summaries.activity:
- **GC safety fix** for `ocaml_uring_setup` landed same-day after [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) reported that the `polling_timeout` parameter was missing from `CAMLparam` registration, merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136), closes [#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135))
- **Build process documentation and simplification** merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), with a note that the subtree scheme was previously broken and a follow-up update was already planned ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137))
- **Vendored liburing updated to 2.14** in a large squashed subtree merge, followed immediately by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) adding new operation and flags enums to the OCaml wrappers ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- **Missing `send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` ops added** to `discover.ml` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), which were absent from the capability discovery list despite being present in liburing ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
summaries.discussions_summary: Vesa Karvonen identified and reported the GC registration bug, which was promptly acknowledged and fixed.
summaries.notable_discussions: - [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) filed [#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135) identifying that `ocaml_uring_setup` used `CAMLparam1(entries)` but the `polling_timeout` argument (an `int option`) was not registered, making it unsafe under GC pressure; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) responded with a fix PR within the hour and the issue was closed the same day ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136))
summaries.trends_summary: Rapid tracking of upstream liburing releases with same-week OCaml API surface updates for newly exposed operations.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- The **liburing 2.14 update** followed by immediate additions to `discover.ml` and `uring.mli` demonstrates a pattern of keeping the OCaml bindings closely aligned with upstream liburing releases, exposing new kernel capabilities quickly ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138), [#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
- **Zero-copy networking operations** (`send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc`, `recv_zc`) are being actively surfaced in the OCaml API, reflecting growing interest in high-performance I/O paths ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139), [#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
summaries.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard drove the liburing 2.14 update and GC fix; Anil Madhavapeddy added missing zero-copy ops; Vesa Karvonen reported the GC bug.
summaries.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened and merged three PRs: the `ocaml_uring_setup` GC registration fix ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136)), build process documentation improvements ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137)), and the liburing 2.14 vendor update with accompanying OCaml API additions ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened and merged a PR adding the missing `send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` operation entries to the capability discovery system ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
- [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) reported the GC registration bug in `ocaml_uring_setup` with a precise code reference, prompting the same-day fix ([#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135))

summaries.brief_summary: New Uring.Res module for type-safe result handling, CLOEXEC fix for recvmsg FDs, and ZFS write_fixed warning added.
summaries.new_features_summary: Uring.Res module added with int/fd conversion helpers and pretty-printer; MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC applied atomically when receiving FDs.
summaries.new_features:
- New **`Uring.Res` module** providing a `private int` type for uring operation results, replacing raw `int` returns in the `completion_option` type. The module includes `int_result`/`int_exn` for numeric results, `fd_result`/`fd_exn` for file descriptor results, and a `pp` pretty-printer that emits symbolic error names (e.g. `ENOENT`) rather than negative integers — resolving [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) without requiring `Obj.magic` in user code ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131))
- **`MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`** flag is now set atomically when receiving file descriptors via `recvmsg`, preventing FD leakage to child processes in multi-threaded programs ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132))
- **`write_fixed` documentation** now includes a warning about a hang bug affecting old ZFS versions, linking to the tracking issue ([#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133), closes [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))
summaries.activity_summary: Four PRs merged covering the new Res API, a CLOEXEC bug fix, ZFS documentation, and a test hang fix; two long-standing draft PRs closed.
summaries.activity:
- **`Uring.Res` module** merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), drawing on earlier prototype work by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) in [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130); the adopted approach uses a `private int` to avoid extra C call overhead while still providing type-safe fd and error conversions ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131))
- **CLOEXEC bug fix** for `recvmsg`-based FD passing merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), addressing a race condition in multi-threaded programs where a child process could inherit FDs before the flag was set manually ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132))
- **ZFS warning documentation** added to `write_fixed` API, prompted by a suggestion from [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal), closing the long-standing ZFS spin-hang bug report ([#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133), closes [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))
- **Test hang fix** on Linux 5.10 merged: when the sending side of a test fails, the test no longer waits for a receive that will never arrive ([#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134))
- **Two draft PRs closed** without merging: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) (fd-from-int via extra C call) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) (fully typed wait results encoding type in job ID), both superseded by the lighter-weight approach in [#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)
- **Three old issues closed**: WSL2 `sendmsg` failure [#55](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/55) closed as presumed resolved by WSL kernel updates; `Uring.accept` unusable FD [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) closed as fixed by [#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131); ZFS write hang [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113) closed with documentation warning in [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)
summaries.discussions_summary: Design discussion on type-safe result handling compared three approaches before settling on the private-int Res module.
summaries.notable_discussions: - **Type-safe result API design** debated across three PRs: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) added an extra C call to convert ints to FDs safely; [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) explored encoding the expected return type in 4 bits of the job ID so C stubs could return typed OCaml blocks. [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noted in [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) that the fully-typed approach introduced allocation overhead and complicated Eio integration (unknown error codes becoming `EUNKNOWNERR`, additional match arms per operation), ultimately favoring the `private int` approach in [#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131) as the right balance of safety and compatibility
summaries.trends_summary: API ergonomics and safety improvements are a recurring theme, with several old issues cleared through targeted, minimal-overhead fixes.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Reducing unsafe patterns in the public API**: the `Uring.Res` module ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)) eliminates the need for `Obj.magic` when converting uring result integers to `Unix.file_descr`, continuing a broader push to make the library's surface safe by default without imposing runtime overhead
- **Proactive OS-compatibility hardening**: the `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` fix ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132)) and the Linux 5.10 test hang fix ([#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134)) reflect attention to correctness across kernel versions and environments beyond the primary development platform
summaries.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard drove all merged code this week; David Allsopp's earlier prototype PRs informed the final API design.
summaries.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened and merged all four PRs this week: the `Uring.Res` module ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)), the `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` bug fix ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132)), the ZFS documentation warning ([#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)), and the test hang fix ([#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134)); also closed issues [#55](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/55), [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113), and [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) and provided detailed review commentary on the alternative approaches in [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) authored the two earlier prototype PRs [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) that directly shaped the final `Uring.Res` design, including exploration of encoding type information in job IDs and the suggestion to store types on the OCaml side to avoid allocation
- [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) suggested adding a ZFS warning to the `write_fixed` documentation, which led to [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)

summaries.brief_summary: Design discussions on file descriptor representation and type safety in the OCaml bindings
summaries.activity_summary: Two open PRs exploring file descriptor type safety with active design discussions
summaries.activity:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened two PRs exploring **type-safe file descriptor handling** in the library: [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) eliminates `Obj.magic` by handling `AT_FDCWD` in C stubs, while draft [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130) proposes more invasive changes to introduce type-safe wait functions that differentiate between different return types
- Extended discussion on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) involving [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) debated the **abstraction boundaries** between OCaml and C representations of file descriptors, with references to David's blog post on [File descriptors are not integers](https://www.dra27.uk/blog/platform/2025/09/30/file-descriptors-are-not-integers.html) and upstream OCaml PR [ocaml/ocaml#14258](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14258)
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) indicated readiness to merge [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) pending end-to-end testing with Eio, while discussion continued on optimal approaches for **flambda optimization** of optional arguments and potential deeper integration into the Eio Linux backend
summaries.discussions_summary: Architecture debate on file descriptor representation and C/OCaml boundary design
summaries.notable_discussions:
- **File descriptor abstraction design** sparked debate on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) about whether OCaml code should access underlying integer representations: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) argued that kernel interfaces require integer access and avoiding it pushes dangerous code into C, while [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) contended that exposing OCaml integer representations is dangerous because they differ from C integers, with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) supporting abstract types with safe conversions for Unix.file_descr, C bindings, and logging
- **Windows vs Linux portability** briefly discussed on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130), with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noting file descriptors are definitively integers on Linux while genuinely different on Windows, and [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) mentioning Windows ioring differences would warrant a separate library with Eio integration
- **Compiler optimization** of optional arguments discussed on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128), with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noting that flambda 1 and 2 optimize optional arguments better than the current approach suggests, prompting plans to refine the implementation
summaries.trends_summary: Focus on type safety improvements and eliminating unsafe operations
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Eliminating Obj.magic** usage emerged as a priority across the codebase, with [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130) both targeting removal of unsafe operations, motivated by upstream work in [ocaml/ocaml#14258](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14258)
- **Type safety vs allocation trade-offs** became a central design consideration, with discussions on [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130) exploring how to differentiate return types without increasing allocations on the fast path
- **Linux-specific optimizations** gaining attention, with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggesting on [#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128) that `private int` types for file descriptors and deeper Eio_linux integration could reduce Unix.file_descr usage until necessary

summaries.brief_summary: Three PRs opened addressing type safety and eliminating Obj.magic in file descriptor handling
summaries.activity_summary: Type safety improvements for file descriptor handling with alternative approaches under discussion
summaries.activity:
- Three **new PRs opened** this week by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) focusing on type safety and eliminating unsafe code: type-safe wait functions ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130)), safer file descriptor wizardry ([#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/129)), and handling `AT_FDCWD` in C to eliminate `Obj.magic` ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128))
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) proposed **type-safe wait functions** as a more invasive alternative to address issue [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117), ensuring OCaml ints only represent actual integers rather than encoded values, with no allocation difference for the fast path and no cost for retrieving `Unix.file_descr` values from syscalls returning descriptors ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- Design discussion emerged around **file descriptor abstraction** between [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) exploring whether file descriptors should be treated as abstract types rather than integers, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noting similarities to Eio's existing approach in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- Alternative approach in [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/129) provides simpler solution to [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) at the cost of an extra C call, currently on hold pending resolution of design discussion in [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130)
summaries.discussions_summary: Design debate on treating file descriptors as abstract types versus integers across platforms
summaries.notable_discussions:
- **File descriptor type safety** discussion exploring whether OCaml file descriptors should be abstract types, with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noting that while file descriptors are integers on *nix systems, they're not on Windows, and questioning when direct integer access is truly necessary beyond process spawning ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- Platform-specific considerations examined with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) clarifying that OCaml file descriptors are not integers on Windows though C ones are, while OS-level Windows descriptors are pointers, suggesting `type unix_fd = private int` as a potential solution for Linux-only code like ocaml-uring ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- Potential for **Windows support** raised by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noting similarities between liburing and `ioringapi.h`, suggesting ocaml-uring might not need to remain Linux-only ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
summaries.trends_summary: Movement toward stronger type safety and elimination of unsafe code patterns
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Type safety improvements** across the codebase with focus on eliminating `Obj.magic` usage and ensuring proper type representation for system-level abstractions ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/128), [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/129), [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- **Cross-platform considerations** emerging in discussions despite current Linux focus, with exploration of how design decisions might affect potential Windows support ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
- **Alignment with Eio ecosystem** continuing as design patterns in ocaml-uring are evaluated against existing Eio implementations, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) highlighting similar approaches already used in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) ([#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/130))
summaries.contributors_summary: David Allsopp opened three type safety PRs with active design discussions involving Anil Madhavapeddy and Thomas Leonard
summaries.contributors:
